name: Kahurangi 500
description: A demanding route through remote terrain and long climbing days.
difficultyExplanation: Kahurangi 500 mixes remote wilderness riding, serious climbing, and technical trail sections. Limited services and backcountry weather make this one a true hard route.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Wangapeka Track crossing is the most remote stretch with no services for two or more days. Food carry over this section is critical.
routeSummary: Kahurangi 500 is a hard bikepacking route covering about 519 km, commonly ridden over 7 days. A demanding route through remote terrain and long climbing days. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Nov-Mar, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Wangapeka Track crossing is the most remote stretch with no services for two or more days. Food carry over this section is critical. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 7-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
